home.title: Projects
home.subtitle: {count, plural, one {# project} other {# projects}} in your workspace
home.emptyDescription: PuppyOne is your file workspace for multi-agent collaboration. Click below to start your first project.

project.label: Create your first project
project.description: Projects are containers for your data. Click '+ New Project' to get started.

file.label: Upload your first file
file.description: Go to the Context page, drag files or use mut push to sync a local folder.

access_point.label: Create an Access Point
access_point.description: Access Points let Claude, Cursor, and other tools access your data.

local_sync.label: Sync with mut CLI
local_sync.description: Install mut CLI and run mut push for bidirectional real-time sync with PuppyOne.

agent.label: Create an AI Agent
agent.description: Click '+ New Agent' in the right panel of the Context page, then bind data resources.

chat.label: Chat with your Agent
chat.description: Select an Agent and start chatting to let AI help you process data.

invite.label: Invite team members
invite.description: Invite members on the team page to manage projects and Agents together.

welcome.slide0Subtitle: Store your data in PuppyOne so AI Agents can access it anytime — always knowing what they're working on.
welcome.slide0Caption: From scattered files to unified context
welcome.slide1Subtitle: Local folders, Google Drive, GitHub, web pages… one-click sync with automatic versioning.
welcome.slide2Subtitle: Every Agent and tool can only access the scope you authorize — secure and auditable.

userMenu.manageBilling: Manage your subscription and billing settings.
userMenu.integrationsDescription: Connect your accounts to import data from external services.
userMenu.disconnectWarning: You will need to re-authorize to import data from this service again.

projectDialog.deleteWarning: This permanently deletes the project and every context node inside it. This action cannot be undone.

team.title: Team
team.subtitle: Manage members and their access to {org}.

projectHome.accessPoints: {count, plural, one {# access point} other {# access points}}
